```{r gear-sec13-multipliers}
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(b$pe_fallback) || !b$pe_fallback) {
    B1_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "B1")$B1
    B2_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "B2")$B2

    weekend_mult <- exp(B1_draws)
    holiday_mult <- exp(B1_draws + B2_draws)

    multiplier_results[[label]] <- tibble(
      fit = label,
      B1_median = round(median(B1_draws), 3),
      B1_lo95 = round(quantile(B1_draws, 0.025), 3),
      B1_hi95 = round(quantile(B1_draws, 0.975), 3),
      weekend_multiplier = sprintf("%.1fx (%.1f-%.1f)",
        median(weekend_mult), quantile(weekend_mult, 0.025), quantile(weekend_mult, 0.975)),
      B2_median = round(median(B2_draws), 3),
      B2_lo95 = round(quantile(B2_draws, 0.025), 3),
      B2_hi95 = round(quantile(B2_draws, 0.975), 3),
      holiday_multiplier = sprintf("%.1fx (%.1f-%.1f)",
        median(holiday_mult), quantile(holiday_mult, 0.025), quantile(holiday_mult, 0.975))
    )
  }
}
if(length(multiplier_results) > 0) {
  multiplier_df <- bind_rows(multiplier_results)
  message("\n  Effort multipliers relative to weekdays:\n")
  for(i in 1:nrow(multiplier_df)) {
    message(sprintf("    %s:\n      Weekends: %s\n      Holidays: %s\n",
                multiplier_df$fit[i],
                multiplier_df$weekend_multiplier[i],
                multiplier_df$holiday_multiplier[i]))
  }
  write.csv(multiplier_df, file.path(output_dir, "effort_multipliers.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}


# ===========================================================================
# 7.15 INTERVIEW CPUE SUMMARY BY MONTH x POPULATION
# ===========================================================================

message("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n INTERVIEW CPUE SUMMARY\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

cpue_summary <- dwg$interview |>
  filter(fishing_time_total > 0, population %in% c("shore","private_boat")) |>
  mutate(
    month_label = format(event_date, "%Y-%m"),
    cpue = dungeness_kept / fishing_time_total
  ) |>
  group_by(population, month_label) |>
  summarise(
    n_interviews = n(),
    mean_cpue = round(mean(cpue, na.rm=TRUE), 3),
    sd_cpue = round(sd(cpue, na.rm=TRUE), 3),
    se_cpue = round(sd(cpue, na.rm=TRUE) / sqrt(n()), 3),
    median_cpue = round(median(cpue, na.rm=TRUE), 3),
    mean_dungeness = round(mean(dungeness_kept, na.rm=TRUE), 1),
    mean_hours = round(mean(fishing_time_total, na.rm=TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(population, month_label)

message("\n")
report_table(cpue_summary, caption = "Interview CPUE summary by month")
write.csv(cpue_summary, file.path(output_dir, "interview_cpue_summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)



```

## 14. Expansion ratios (R_G and R_G_boat)

The gear-per-crabber ratio (shore) and gear-per-boat-group ratio (boat) that carry effort onto the gear-deployment scale. **Healthy:** the shore R_G is data-identified (a tight interval near ~1.3 gear per crabber); the boat R_G is prior-driven (no gear counts in boat fits), so read its wide interval as expected, not a failure.

```{r gear-sec14-expansion}
# ===========================================================================
# 7.16 R_G AND R_G_boat POSTERIOR SUMMARIES
# v5.3: Stan model now exposes R_G_boat (gear per boat group) in place of
#       R_T (trailers per group). For shore fits R_G_boat is sampled from
#       its prior but has no data attached, so it is reported only for
#       boat fits. For boat fits R_G is fixed at params$R_G_fixed_boat.
# v5.1: Notes R_G behavior for boat vs shore fits
# ===========================================================================

message("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n EXPANSION RATIO POSTERIORS (R_G, R_G_boat)\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

expansion_results <- list()
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(b$pe_fallback) || !b$pe_fallback) {
    R_G_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "R_G")$R_G

    # R_G_boat is only data-informed for boat fits; report it there.
    # F5: R_G is NEVER fixed. estimate_R_G and R_G_fixed are not declared in the
    # Stan model (rstan ignores them), so R_G is always a parameter. In a boat fit
    # Gear_n = 0, so R_G has no likelihood and simply samples its lognormal prior.
    # The old label said "fixed at 1.30", which was false.
    is_shore_fit <- grepl("^shore_", label)
    R_G_status <- if(is_shore_fit) "estimated (gear counts)" else "prior-only (no gear counts in boat fits)"

    if(!is_shore_fit) {
      R_G_boat_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "R_G_boat")$R_G_boat
      R_G_boat_median <- round(median(R_G_boat_draws), 3)
      R_G_boat_lo95   <- round(quantile(R_G_boat_draws, 0.025), 3)
      R_G_boat_hi95   <- round(quantile(R_G_boat_draws, 0.975), 3)
      R_G_boat_interp <- sprintf("%.2f gear per boat group (%.2f-%.2f)",
        R_G_boat_median, R_G_boat_lo95, R_G_boat_hi95)
    } else {
      R_G_boat_median <- NA_real_
      R_G_boat_lo95   <- NA_real_
      R_G_boat_hi95   <- NA_real_
      R_G_boat_interp <- "not applicable (shore fit)"
    }

    expansion_results[[label]] <- tibble(
      fit = label,
      R_G_median = round(median(R_G_draws), 3),
      R_G_lo95 = round(quantile(R_G_draws, 0.025), 3),
      R_G_hi95 = round(quantile(R_G_draws, 0.975), 3),
      R_G_status = R_G_status,
      R_G_interpretation = sprintf("%.2f gear per crabber (%.2f-%.2f) [%s]",
        median(R_G_draws), quantile(R_G_draws, 0.025), quantile(R_G_draws, 0.975), R_G_status),
      R_G_boat_median = R_G_boat_median,
      R_G_boat_lo95 = R_G_boat_lo95,
      R_G_boat_hi95 = R_G_boat_hi95,
      R_G_boat_interpretation = R_G_boat_interp
    )
  }
}
if(length(expansion_results) > 0) {
  expansion_df <- bind_rows(expansion_results)
  message("\n")
  for(i in 1:nrow(expansion_df)) {
    message(sprintf("  %s:\n    R_G:      %s\n    R_G_boat: %s\n",
                expansion_df$fit[i],
                expansion_df$R_G_interpretation[i],
                expansion_df$R_G_boat_interpretation[i]))
  }
  write.csv(expansion_df, file.path(output_dir, "expansion_ratios.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}



```

## 15. PE vs BSS comparison

Side by side, the design-based PE and the Bayesian BSS for each component. **How to read it:** close agreement is reassuring; a large gap on a component means the two methods disagree there, usually because that component is data-thin, and the BSS interval width tells you how well-identified it is. Since both run on the same effort unit, a remaining gap is a genuine model disagreement, not a unit artifact.

```{r gear-sec15-pevsbss}
# ===========================================================================
# 7.17 PE VS BSS COMPARISON TABLE
# ===========================================================================

message("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n PE VS BSS COMPARISON (by component)\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

comparison <- list()
for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
  for(ss in subseasons) {
    label_cg <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name, "_Dungeness_Kept")
    pe_label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name)
    b <- bss_all[[label_cg]]

    pe_catch <- pe_all[[pe_label]]$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0
    pe_effort <- pe_all[[pe_label]]$effort_total %||% 0

    if(!is.null(b) && (is.null(b$pe_fallback) || !b$pe_fallback)) {
      bss_catch <- b$C_sum["50%"]
      bss_effort <- b$E_sum["50%"]
      catch_diff_pct <- round((bss_catch - pe_catch) / pe_catch * 100, 1)
      effort_diff_pct <- round((bss_effort - pe_effort) / pe_effort * 100, 1)

      conv <- convergence_df |> filter(fit == label_cg)
      method <- if(nrow(conv) > 0 && conv$pass_convergence[1]) "BSS" else "PE"
    } else {
      bss_catch <- NA; bss_effort <- NA
      catch_diff_pct <- NA; effort_diff_pct <- NA
      method <- "PE"
    }

    comparison[[length(comparison)+1]] <- tibble(
      component = paste0(pop, " (", ss$display_name, ")"),
      PE_effort = round(pe_effort),
      BSS_effort = round(bss_effort),
      effort_diff_pct = effort_diff_pct,
      PE_catch = round(pe_catch),
      BSS_catch = round(bss_catch),
      catch_diff_pct = catch_diff_pct,
      method_selected = method
    )
  }
}

comparison[[length(comparison)+1]] <- tibble(
  component = "comm_charter (census)",
  PE_effort = round(pe_all$comm_charter$effort_total),
  BSS_effort = NA, effort_diff_pct = NA,
  PE_catch = round(pe_all$comm_charter$Dungeness_Kept),
  BSS_catch = NA, catch_diff_pct = NA,
  method_selected = "Census"
)

comparison_df <- bind_rows(comparison)
message("\n")
report_table(comparison_df, caption = "PE vs BSS by component")
write.csv(comparison_df, file.path(output_dir, "pe_vs_bss_comparison.csv"), row.names = FALSE)



```

## 16. Season summary

Run-level metadata: the season window, sampling coverage, interview count, how many fits passed the gate, and the mode split, a one-page header for the run.

```{r gear-sec16-season}
# ===========================================================================
# 7.18 SEASON SUMMARY ONE-PAGER
# ===========================================================================

message("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n SEASON SUMMARY\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

port_total_cg <- "Dungeness_Kept"
pt_file <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("port_total_", port_total_cg, ".csv"))
if(file.exists(pt_file)) {
  port_total <- read.csv(pt_file)
} else {
  port_total <- tibble(Estimate = c("Effort","Catch"), PE = NA, BSS_median = NA, BSS_lo95 = NA, BSS_hi95 = NA)
}

mode_catch <- c(
  Shore = round(pe_pop_sum("shore", "Dungeness_Kept")),
  `Private Boat` = round(pe_pop_sum("private_boat", "Dungeness_Kept")),
  `Commercial/Charter` = round(pe_all$comm_charter$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0)
)
mode_pct <- round(mode_catch / sum(mode_catch) * 100, 1)

monthly_file <- file.path(output_dir, "monthly_estimates.csv")
top_months <- if(file.exists(monthly_file)) {
  read.csv(monthly_file) |>
    arrange(desc(BSS_Catch_median)) |>
    head(5) |>
    mutate(label = sprintf("%s: %s crab", Month, format(BSS_Catch_median, big.mark=","))) |>
    pull(label)
} else { "Monthly estimates not available" }

n_bss_fits <- sum(!sapply(bss_all, function(b) !is.null(b$pe_fallback) && b$pe_fallback))
n_pass <- sum(convergence_df$pass_convergence, na.rm=TRUE)

total_effort_days <- n_distinct(c(dwg$shore_effort$event_date, dwg$boat_effort$event_date))
total_interview_n <- nrow(dwg$interview)
season_days <- as.integer(as.Date(params$est_date_end) - as.Date(params$est_date_start) + 1)

message("\n  ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n")
message("  ║         SEASON SUMMARY: 2024-25 GRAYS HARBOR        ║\n")
message("  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣\n")
message(sprintf("  ║  Season: %s to %s (%d days)   ║\n",
            params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end, season_days))
message("  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣\n")
message("  ║  PORT TOTAL (Dungeness Kept)                        ║\n")
if(nrow(port_total) >= 2) {
  message(sprintf("  ║    PE:  %s crab                              ║\n",
              format(port_total$PE[2], big.mark=",")))
  if(!is.na(port_total$BSS_median[2])) {
    message(sprintf("  ║    BSS: %s crab (%s - %s)        ║\n",
                format(port_total$BSS_median[2], big.mark=","),
                format(port_total$BSS_lo95[2], big.mark=","),
                format(port_total$BSS_hi95[2], big.mark=",")))
  }
}
message("  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣\n")
message("  ║  HARVEST BY MODE                                    ║\n")
for(nm in names(mode_catch)) {
  message(sprintf("  ║    %-20s %6s (%4.1f%%)             ║\n",
              nm, format(mode_catch[nm], big.mark=","), mode_pct[nm]))
}
message("  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣\n")
message("  ║  TOP HARVEST MONTHS                                 ║\n")
for(m in top_months[1:min(5, length(top_months))]) {
  message(sprintf("  ║    %s\n", m))
}
message("  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣\n")
message("  ║  DATA QUALITY                                       ║\n")
message(sprintf("  ║    Effort sampling: %d of %d days (%d%%)            ║\n",
            total_effort_days, season_days, round(total_effort_days/season_days*100)))
message(sprintf("  ║    Interviews: %s total                        ║\n",
            format(total_interview_n, big.mark=",")))
message(sprintf("  ║    BSS convergence: %d of %d fits passed          ║\n",
            n_pass, n_bss_fits))
message("  ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n")

season_summary <- tibble(
  metric = c("Season start", "Season end", "Season days",
             "PE total catch", "BSS total catch (median)", "BSS 95% CI lower", "BSS 95% CI upper",
             paste("Catch (PE):", names(mode_catch)), paste("Catch % (PE):", names(mode_catch)),
             "Effort days sampled", "Total interviews",
             "BSS fits attempted", "BSS fits passed convergence"),
  value = c(params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end, as.character(season_days),
            if(nrow(port_total)>=2) as.character(c(port_total$PE[2], port_total$BSS_median[2],
              port_total$BSS_lo95[2], port_total$BSS_hi95[2])) else rep(NA, 4),
            as.character(mode_catch), as.character(mode_pct),
            as.character(total_effort_days), as.character(total_interview_n),
            as.character(n_bss_fits), as.character(n_pass))
)
write.csv(season_summary, file.path(output_dir, "season_summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

report_table(season_summary,
  caption = "Season summary. The headline is the BSS gated total; the by-mode Catch (PE) rows are design-based Point Estimates and sum to the PE total, not the BSS total.")

message("\n  New output tables saved.\n")



```

## 17. Incomplete-trip sensitivity

How much dropping incomplete trips (gear not yet retrieved at the interview) moves each component's PE. **Healthy:** dropping incomplete trips raises the estimate (soaking gear reads low mid-trip); a component that moves the other way is worth a second look.

```{r gear-sec17-sensitivity}
# ===========================================================================
# 7.19 SENSITIVITY: INCOMPLETE TRIP EFFECT ON PE ESTIMATES
#
# v5.2: Compares PE estimates with and without the incomplete trip filter
#        to quantify the harvest impact. Reports per-population, per-sub-season,
#        and per-gear-type CPUE differences.
# ===========================================================================

timer_start("7_sensitivity")
message("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n SENSITIVITY: INCOMPLETE TRIP EFFECT\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")

sensitivity_results <- list()

for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
  for(ss in subseasons) {
    label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name)
    days_ss <- prep_days_crab(ss$start, ss$end, params, L_eff_model)
    summ_ss <- prep_population_summary(dwg, pop, ss$start, ss$end, params)

    # Count trip status
    n_total_int <- nrow(summ_ss$interview)
    n_complete <- sum(summ_ss$interview$trip_status == "Complete", na.rm=TRUE)
    n_incomplete <- sum(summ_ss$interview$trip_status == "Incomplete", na.rm=TRUE)
    n_missing_ts <- sum(is.na(summ_ss$interview$trip_status))

    # PE with all trips
    params_all <- params; params_all$filter_incomplete_trips <- FALSE
    pe_all_trips <- run_pe_gear(summ_ss, days_ss, params_all, paste0(label, " [all trips]"),
                           population = pop)

    # PE with completed trips only
    params_comp <- params; params_comp$filter_incomplete_trips <- TRUE
    summ_comp <- summ_ss
    summ_comp$interview <- summ_ss$interview |>
      filter(trip_status == "Complete" | is.na(trip_status))
    pe_complete <- run_pe_gear(summ_comp, days_ss, params_comp, paste0(label, " [complete only]"),
                          population = pop)

    diff_pct <- if(pe_all_trips$Dungeness_Kept > 0) {
      (pe_complete$Dungeness_Kept - pe_all_trips$Dungeness_Kept) /
        pe_all_trips$Dungeness_Kept * 100
    } else { NA }

    message(sprintf("  %s: All=%s, Complete=%s (diff=%+.1f%%)\n",
                label,
                format(round(pe_all_trips$Dungeness_Kept), big.mark=","),
                format(round(pe_complete$Dungeness_Kept), big.mark=","),
                diff_pct))

    sensitivity_results[[length(sensitivity_results)+1]] <- tibble(
      component = label,
      n_interviews_total = n_total_int,
      n_complete = n_complete,
      n_incomplete = n_incomplete,
      n_missing_status = n_missing_ts,
      pct_incomplete = round(n_incomplete / max(n_complete + n_incomplete, 1) * 100, 1),
      PE_catch_all_trips = round(pe_all_trips$Dungeness_Kept),
      PE_catch_complete_only = round(pe_complete$Dungeness_Kept),
      diff_pct = round(diff_pct, 1),
      filter_applied = isTRUE(params$filter_incomplete_trips)
    )
  }
}

sensitivity_df <- bind_rows(sensitivity_results)
message("\n")
report_table(sensitivity_df, caption = "Incomplete-trip sensitivity (PE catch)")
write.csv(sensitivity_df, file.path(output_dir, "sensitivity_incomplete_trips.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Gear-type-specific CPUE comparison (shore only, all sub-seasons combined)
message("\n--- Gear-Type CPUE: Complete vs Incomplete (Shore) ---\n")
shore_int <- dwg$interview |>
  filter(population == "shore",
         between(event_date, as.Date(params$est_date_start), as.Date(params$est_date_end)),
         fishing_time_total >= 0.5) |>
  mutate(
    cpue = dungeness_kept / fishing_time_total,
    has_pot      = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bpot\\b") &
                              !str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bslip\\s*ring\\b")),
    has_ring_net = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bring\\s*net\\b")),
    has_trap     = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\b(trap|star)\\b")),
    has_snare    = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bsnare\\b")),
    n_gear_types = pmax(has_pot + has_ring_net + has_trap + has_snare, 1L)
  )

gear_sensitivity <- list()
for(gname in c("Pot","Ring Net","Trap","Snare")) {
  gcol <- c(Pot="has_pot", `Ring Net`="has_ring_net", Trap="has_trap", Snare="has_snare")[gname]
  gdata <- shore_int |> filter(.data[[gcol]] == 1, n_gear_types == 1)  # single-gear only
  if(nrow(gdata) < 10) next
  by_status <- gdata |>
    group_by(trip_status) |>
    summarise(mean_cpue = mean(cpue), n = n(), .groups = "drop")
  cpue_c <- by_status |> filter(trip_status == "Complete") |> pull(mean_cpue)
  cpue_i <- by_status |> filter(trip_status == "Incomplete") |> pull(mean_cpue)
  n_c <- by_status |> filter(trip_status == "Complete") |> pull(n)
  n_i <- by_status |> filter(trip_status == "Incomplete") |> pull(n)
  if(length(cpue_c) > 0 && length(cpue_i) > 0 && cpue_c > 0) {
    bias <- (cpue_i - cpue_c) / cpue_c * 100
    message(sprintf("  %10s: Complete=%.3f (n=%d), Incomplete=%.3f (n=%d), Bias=%+.1f%%\n",
                gname, cpue_c, n_c, cpue_i, n_i, bias))
    gear_sensitivity[[length(gear_sensitivity)+1]] <- tibble(
      gear_type = gname, cpue_complete = round(cpue_c, 3), n_complete = n_c,
      cpue_incomplete = round(cpue_i, 3), n_incomplete = n_i,
      bias_pct = round(bias, 1)
    )
  }
}
if(length(gear_sensitivity) > 0) {
  gear_sens_df <- bind_rows(gear_sensitivity)
  write.csv(gear_sens_df, file.path(output_dir, "sensitivity_incomplete_by_gear.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}
timer_stop("7_sensitivity")


# ---- 7.20 SAVE PARAMETERS ----
timer_stop("6_outputs")
writeLines(capture.output(str(params)), file.path(output_dir, "run_parameters.txt"))

message(sprintf("\nAll outputs saved to: %s\n", output_dir))
message(paste(" ", list.files(output_dir), collapse="\n"), "\n")

# ---- TIMING SUMMARY ----
run_total <- difftime(Sys.time(), run_start_time, units = "mins")
message("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n TIMING SUMMARY\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
timing_df <- tibble(
  Section = names(bss_timer_log()),
  Minutes = sapply(bss_timer_log(), function(x) round(as.numeric(x$elapsed), 1))
)
report_table(timing_df, caption = "Run timing summary")
message(sprintf("\n  TOTAL RUNTIME: %.1f minutes (%.1f hours)\n",
            as.numeric(run_total), as.numeric(run_total)/60))

```

## Model diagnostics (tables)

Per-fit diagnostic tables, surfaced on the page so the gear-resolved report matches the pooled driver's HTML output. Each table reads a CSV the run already wrote via the shared writers in `03_R_functions/` (during the driver chunk above); a missing file renders nothing rather than interrupting the report. These are display-only and change no estimate.

### Per-fit convergence summary

```{r gear-convergence-report-show, echo=FALSE}
# Curated, HTML-friendly view of the convergence report: short fit labels and the key
# gate columns only. The full table (treedepth, per-criterion pass flags, level
# distortion) is written to convergence_report.csv. convergence_df is assembled in the
# driver chunk above. Gear-resolved keeps one extra column the pooled table lacks,
# max gear R-hat, because per-gear-type convergence is the point of this track.
conv_disp <- tryCatch(
  dplyr::transmute(convergence_df,
    Fit              = short_fit(fit),
    AR               = ar_resolution,
    Div              = divergences,
    `Div frac`       = round(divergence_fraction, 3),
    `R-hat C`        = round(C_sum_rhat, 3),
    `R-hat E`        = round(E_sum_rhat, 3),
    `max gear R-hat` = round(max_gear_rhat, 3),
    `min n_eff`      = pmin(C_sum_neff, E_sum_neff),
    `Impact C (SD)`  = round(impact_C_sd, 3),
    `Impact E (SD)`  = round(impact_E_sd, 3),
    Pass             = pass_convergence,
    Method           = method_selected),
  error = function(e) convergence_df)
report_table(conv_disp,
  paste("Per-fit convergence summary (key gate columns; full detail in",
        "convergence_report.csv). Div frac is the divergent-transition fraction (backstop",
        "0.05); Impact C/E (SD) is the scale-aware gating criterion (threshold 0.10);",
        "max gear R-hat is the worst per-gear-type R-hat (gear-resolved only); Method is",
        "what the totals actually use. Healthy: R-hat near 1.00, min n_eff above 400,",
        "Pass = TRUE."),
  digits = 3)
```

### Posterior-predictive calibration

Posterior-predictive coverage and PIT for each fit and data stream, written per fit during the run.

```{r gear-diag-perfit-show, echo=FALSE}
# Surface the posterior-predictive calibration written above so the health check is on
# the page, not only in ppc_calibration_<fit>.csv. Healthy: 95% coverage near 0.95 and
# PIT mean near 0.50; a coverage far from nominal or a PIT far from 0.50 flags a
# mis-calibrated (usually over-dispersed) predictive for that data stream.
ppc <- read_fit_outputs("ppc_calibration")
ppc_disp <- if (!is.null(ppc)) tryCatch(
  dplyr::transmute(ppc,
    Fit         = fit_lbl,
    Stream      = data_type,
    `Cover 50%` = round(coverage_50, 2),
    `Cover 95%` = round(coverage_95, 2),
    `PIT mean`  = round(pit_mean, 2),
    `PIT sd`    = round(pit_sd, 2),
    n           = n_obs),
  error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
report_table(ppc_disp,
  paste("Posterior-predictive calibration by fit and data stream. Healthy: Cover 95%",
        "near 0.95 and PIT mean near 0.50; coverage well above nominal means the",
        "predictive is too wide (over-dispersed), the effort streams are the known case."))
```

### Effort over-dispersion decomposition

Splits each effort-count predictive variance into its Poisson floor, negative-binomial observation over-dispersion, and latent-process parts, per fit.

```{r gear-diag-overdisp-show, echo=FALSE}
# Surface the effort over-dispersion decomposition written above. Shares sum to 1 and
# name the lever for any excess effort variance: the Poisson floor, NB observation
# over-dispersion (r_E / sigma_r_E), or latent AR-process variance (sigma_eps_E).
od <- read_fit_outputs("effort_overdispersion_decomp")
od_disp <- if (!is.null(od)) tryCatch(
  dplyr::transmute(od,
    Fit           = fit_lbl,
    Stream        = data_type,
    Poisson       = round(share_poisson, 2),
    `NB overdisp` = round(share_nb_overdisp, 2),
    `Latent AR`   = round(share_latent, 2),
    `Cover 50%`   = round(ppc_coverage_50, 2)),
  error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
report_table(od_disp,
  paste("Effort-count predictive variance decomposition (shares sum to 1): Poisson floor,",
        "negative-binomial observation over-dispersion, and latent AR-process variance.",
        "The dominant share is the lever to tune if the effort predictive is too wide."))
```

### Extended per-fit outputs and PSIS-LOO

CPUE effort-unit validity, per-fit data coverage, and PSIS-LOO cross-validation, read back from the CSVs written during the run.

```{r gear-diag-extended-show, echo=FALSE}
# (a) CPUE estimator triad + linearity + saturation: is the effort unit harvest-valid?
.est <- read_fit_outputs("cpue_estimators")
.lin <- read_fit_outputs("cpue_linearity")
.sat <- read_fit_outputs("cpue_saturation")
cpue_disp <- if (!is.null(.est)) tryCatch({
  d <- dplyr::select(.est, fit_lbl, effort_unit, n_obs, cpue_ratio_of_sums, cpue_model_implied)
  if (!is.null(.lin)) d <- dplyr::left_join(d, dplyr::select(.lin, fit_lbl, beta_h, linearity_flag), by = "fit_lbl")
  if (!is.null(.sat)) d <- dplyr::left_join(d, dplyr::select(.sat, fit_lbl, sat_beta = beta, saturation_flag), by = "fit_lbl")
  dplyr::transmute(d,
    Fit          = fit_lbl,
    Unit         = effort_unit,
    n            = n_obs,
    `CPUE RoS`   = round(cpue_ratio_of_sums, 2),
    `CPUE model` = round(cpue_model_implied, 2),
    beta_h       = round(beta_h, 2),
    `linear?`    = dplyr::if_else(linearity_flag, "FLAG", "ok", missing = "-"),
    `sat beta`   = round(sat_beta, 2),
    `sat?`       = dplyr::if_else(saturation_flag, "FLAG", "ok", missing = "-"))
}, error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
report_table(cpue_disp,
  paste("CPUE effort-unit validity. beta_h is the catch-vs-effort elasticity (harvest-",
        "unbiased when it covers 1, shown as 'linear? = ok'); the model CPUE should track",
        "the design-based ratio-of-sums (RoS). 'sat? = FLAG' (catch sub-linear in soak",
        "time) is expected for pots and is the reason the unit is deployments, not hours."))

# (b) Per-fit data coverage: how much the BSS must lean on the process model.
.fds <- tryCatch(utils::read.csv(file.path(output_dir, "fit_data_summary.csv"),
                                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)
fds_disp <- if (!is.null(.fds)) tryCatch(
  dplyr::transmute(.fds,
    Fit          = short_fit(fit),
    Method       = method,
    AR           = ar_resolution,
    Days         = D,
    `Eff obs`    = n_effort_obs,
    Interviews   = n_interviews,
    `I/E obs`    = n_ie_obs,
    `% days eff` = round(pct_days_with_effort, 0),
    `% days int` = round(pct_days_with_interview, 0)),
  error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
report_table(fds_disp,
  paste("Per-fit data coverage: latent days, effort observations, interviews, and I/E",
        "observations, with the percent of days carrying any effort/interview data. Thin",
        "coverage is where the BSS leans hardest on the AR process and priors."))

# (c) PSIS-LOO by stream: predictive fit and reliability (Pareto k).
.loo <- read_fit_outputs("loo_summary")
loo_disp <- if (!is.null(.loo)) tryCatch(
  dplyr::transmute(.loo,
    Fit             = fit_lbl,
    Stream          = stream,
    n               = n_obs,
    elpd_loo        = round(elpd_loo, 1),
    p_loo           = round(p_loo, 1),
    `Pareto k>0.7`  = `n_pareto_k_gt_0.7`),
  error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
report_table(loo_disp,
  paste("PSIS-LOO by data stream. p_loo is the effective number of parameters; a large",
        "'Pareto k>0.7' count means LOO is unreliable for that stream (the model is near-",
        "saturated there), so read its elpd_loo with caution."))
```

### Summer-extrapolation transparency

How much of each fitted component's BSS catch falls on days with **no interview**, where the CPUE is imputed by the AR process and priors rather than observed. A high extrapolated share means that component's harvest rests on the model filling unsampled time; read it as a coverage diagnostic, not a re-estimate of the total.

```{r gear-extrapolation-transparency, echo=FALSE}
# Decompose each fit's BSS catch into interview-days vs extrapolated (no interview) days,
# from the persisted daily catch (bss_daily_catch_*) and daily CPUE (bss_daily_cpue_*,
# which carries n_interviews) outputs. A day is "extrapolated" when it has zero
# interviews, so its CPUE comes entirely from the AR process and priors. Diagnostic only;
# writes extrapolation_transparency.csv.
.dc <- read_fit_outputs("bss_daily_catch")
.dq <- read_fit_outputs("bss_daily_cpue")
extrap_tbl <- NULL
if (!is.null(.dc) && !is.null(.dq) && "n_interviews" %in% names(.dq)) {
  extrap_tbl <- tryCatch({
    dplyr::left_join(
      dplyr::transmute(.dc, fit_lbl, event_date = as.character(event_date), catch = .data[["median"]]),
      dplyr::transmute(.dq, fit_lbl, event_date = as.character(event_date), n_interviews),
      by = c("fit_lbl", "event_date")) |>
      dplyr::mutate(n_interviews = dplyr::coalesce(n_interviews, 0)) |>
      dplyr::group_by(Fit = fit_lbl) |>
      dplyr::summarise(
        `days total`         = dplyr::n(),
        `days sampled`       = sum(n_interviews > 0),
        `catch total`        = round(sum(catch, na.rm = TRUE)),
        `catch extrapolated` = round(sum(catch[n_interviews == 0], na.rm = TRUE)),
        .groups = "drop") |>
      dplyr::mutate(
        `% days sampled`       = round(100 * `days sampled` / `days total`, 0),
        `% catch extrapolated` = round(100 * `catch extrapolated` / `catch total`, 1))
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
}
if (!is.null(extrap_tbl) && nrow(extrap_tbl) > 0) {
  port_extrap_pct <- round(100 * sum(extrap_tbl$`catch extrapolated`) / sum(extrap_tbl$`catch total`), 1)
  utils::write.csv(extrap_tbl, file.path(output_dir, "extrapolation_transparency.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  report_table(extrap_tbl,
    paste0("Share of each fitted component's BSS catch on days with no interview (CPUE ",
           "imputed by the AR process). Across the fitted components, ", port_extrap_pct,
           "% of the summed BSS catch falls on extrapolated days. A high '% catch ",
           "extrapolated' flags a component whose harvest rests on the model filling ",
           "unsampled time; shares use summed daily medians, so read as a coverage ",
           "diagnostic, not a re-estimate of the total."))
} else {
  message("_Extrapolation transparency unavailable (daily catch / CPUE outputs not found)._")
}
```
